matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules receive the decoded sample GT, zygosity, ALT dosage, and site REF -> ALT definition; curated allele rules take precedence only when the sample genotype carries the relevant allele dosage.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
seeded_markers: GRIN2B neurobiology variation model (GRIN2B neurological and neurodegenerative biology pathway model)
GRIN2B regulatory expression context model (GRIN2B expression and methylation-context model)
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a GRIN2B neurological and neurodegenerative biology thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to synaptic signaling, neurotransmission, protein aggregation, neuronal survival, neurodevelopment, and neurodegeneration biology. Treat the result as candidate pathway evidence until exact allele, zygosity, phenotype, and external classification are reviewed.

variant_prediction_rules.common_name: GRIN2B neurological and neurodegenerative biology pathway model
variant_prediction_rules.prediction: GT-confirmed GRIN2B neurobiology variation model dosage suggests this sample carries the GRIN2B prediction context described by the gene-level thesis. The variant observed in this sample suggests a GRIN2B neurological and neurodegenerative biology thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to synaptic signaling, neurotransmission, protein aggregation, neuronal survival, neurodevelopment, and neurodegeneration biology. Treat the result as candidate pathway evidence until exact allele, zygosity, phenotype, and external classification are reviewed. This specific marker is curated as GRIN2B neurological and neurodegenerative biology pathway model. GRIN2B neurobiology variation model is curated as a GRIN2B marker for Neurological, neurodevelopmental, and neurodegenerative disease context, Synaptic signaling, excitability, neurotransmitter, and protein-homeostasis biology. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the synaptic signaling, neurotransmission, protein aggregation, neuronal survival, neurodevelopment, and neurodegeneration biology pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched GRIN2B neurobiology variation model at site definition {change} with GT {gt_raw} decoded as {genotype} ({zygosity}). Anchor the GRIN2B prediction to genotype dosage {allele_dosage} rather than REF -> ALT presence alone. Dosage-specific conclusions should be softened when GT, depth, balance, or genotype quality are uncertain.
variant_prediction_rules.basis: Can modify or tag synaptic signaling, neurotransmission, protein aggregation, neuronal survival, neurodevelopment, and neurodegeneration biology.

methylation_sources.label: Whitelist mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: Gene-name-related mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses rows whose gene annotation explicitly names the current gene.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a GRIN2B neurological and neurodegenerative biology thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to synaptic signaling, neurotransmission, protein aggregation, neuronal survival, neurodevelopment, and neurodegeneration biology. Treat the result as candidate pathway evidence until exact allele, zygosity, phenotype, and external classification are reviewed. An observed GRIN2B variant places this sample in the curated GRIN2B research context. The local GRIN2B bundle is oriented toward neurological and neurodegenerative biology. Use it for pathway triage, phenotype review, and candidate-gene context; external variant classification is required before clinical interpretation. GRIN2B variants can alter, tag, or contextualize synaptic signaling, neurotransmission, protein aggregation, neuronal survival, neurodevelopment, and neurodegeneration biology.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a GRIN2B variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. The variant side of the thesis is: The variant observed in this sample suggests a GRIN2B neurological and neurodegenerative biology thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to synaptic signaling, neurotransmission, protein aggregation, neuronal survival, neurodevelopment, and neurodegeneration biology. Treat the result as candidate pathway evidence until exact allele, zygosity, phenotype, and external classification are reviewed. GRIN2B methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a synaptic signaling, neurotransmission, protein aggregation, neuronal survival, neurodevelopment, and neurodegeneration biology gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data. The local GRIN2B bundle is oriented toward neurological and neurodegenerative biology. Use it for pathway triage, phenotype review, and candidate-gene context; external variant classification is required before clinical interpretation. GRIN2B variants can alter, tag, or contextualize synaptic signaling, neurotransmission, protein aggregation, neuronal survival, neurodevelopment, and neurodegeneration biology.
